title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Blackley Preston informs Detective Chief Inspector Demott Caddock that Marina Gregg has been under the care of Dr. Morris Gilchrist for several years due to recurring illness, though she regained her health approximately one year ago. At Caddock's request, Preston brings Dr. Gilchrist to meet him, and the two men move to the doctor's private bedroom to speak privately.

Dr. Gilchrist explains that Gregg cannot receive visitors or participate in the investigation because she is suffering from nervous tension; he warns that questioning her would cause her to become hysterical. While he notes she is currently depressed, he suggests she will likely have a strong desire to speak with Caddock within the next forty-eight hours. Providing a psychological profile, Gilchrist describes Gregg as an excellent but emotionally unstable actress whose moods oscillate between ecstasy and despair, noting her life has been marked by pain and unhappy marriages.

When Caddock inquires about Gregg's reaction to the death of Heather Badcock, Gilchrist remains non-committal, suggesting she might react with sincere sympathy, dramatize the event, or react for other unknown reasons. Regarding medication, Gilchrist reveals that Gregg relies on a drug called Camon, which is also commonly used by others in the industry, such as Eira Jelinsky and Harley Preston, to provide calm and energy. Gilchrist concludes by stating he is weighing his conflicting responsibilities between his professional duty to maintain patient confidentiality and his duty to take action if he suspects a patient is in danger.
